question.content: Draw the final shear-force and bending-moment diagrams for the beam. Answer format: one final image only, with no extra text.

step_1.content:
Use whole-beam equilibrium to find reactions.

\(\sum M_A=0\):
\(3\times0.6+3.6-(10\times1.2)\times1.2+F_{RB}\times1.8=0\),
so \(F_{RB}=5\,\text{kN}\).

\(\sum F_y=0\):
\(F_{RA}+F_{RB}-3-10\times1.2=0\),
thus \(F_{RA}=10\,\text{kN}\).
step_1.key_point: First solve support reactions from global force and moment balance.

step_3.content: On CA (0.6 m), there is no distributed load, so shear is constant: \(F_s=-3\,\text{kN}\).
step_3.key_point: No distributed load implies constant shear on CA.

step_4.content: At A, the reaction \(+10\,\text{kN}\) causes a shear jump: \(-3\to+7\,\text{kN}\). On AD (0.6 m), shear stays \(+7\,\text{kN}\).
step_4.key_point: Shear jumps at concentrated force and stays constant between jumps.

step_5.key_point: UDL makes shear a linear function on DB.

step_6.content: At B-: \(F_s=7-10\times1.2=-5\,\text{kN}\). After adding \(F_{RB}=5\,\text{kN}\), shear returns to 0 at B. Zero shear occurs at \(\xi=0.7\,\text{m}\).
step_6.key_point: The \(F_s=0\) position gives the moment extremum location.

step_7.key_point: Moment on CA and AD is obtained by integrating constant shear.

step_8.content: At D, the applied couple gives a jump: \(M_{D^+}=2.4-3.6=-1.2\,\text{kN*m}\). On DB: \(M(\xi)=-1.2+7\xi-5\xi^2\).
step_8.key_point: Applied couple causes finite moment jump; DB moment is parabolic.

step_9.key_point: Use these ordinates to plot the final shear and moment diagrams.
